name: Ciudad de Segovia: the Aqueduct and the Alcázar — circular
name_original: Ciudad de Segovia: el Aqueducto y el Alcázar — circular
region_intro: Hiking around Segovia offers diverse landscapes, from dramatic river gorges to towering mountain peaks and serene river valleys. The region is characterized by limestone cliffs, dense pine forests, and a network of trails winding through historical areas. Hikers can explore the natural beauty of the Hoces del Río Duratón Natural Park and the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park. Best hiking trails around Segovia The most popular hiking route is Ciudad de Segovia: the Aqueduct and the Alcázar — circular, a 2.9 miles (4.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 18 minutes to complete. This easy route offers views of Segovia's iconic historical monuments. Another top favourite among local hikers is Río Eresma with views of the Alcázar de Segòvia — circular, a moderate 4.9 miles (7.9 km) path. This trail follows the Eresma River, providing scenic views of the Alcázar de Segovia. Local hikers also love the Pinar de la Acebeda y El Berrueco — circular por el Acueducto en Revenga: Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Guadarrama, a 9.4 miles (15.1 km) trail leading through pine forests and past the aqueduct, often completed in about 5 hours 13 minutes. Hiking around Segovia is defined by river gorges, mountain peaks, and dense pine forests. The network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from easy riverside strolls to challenging mountain climbs. The routes in Segovia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 190 reviews. More than 1200 hikers have used komoot to explore Segovia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Segovia?
A: There are over 60 hiking trails around Segovia, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix, with about 20 easy routes, 34 moderate, and 6 more challenging options.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Segovia?
A: Yes, Segovia offers several easy trails perfect for beginners and families. A great option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153091765/ciudad-de-segovia-the-aqueduct-and-the-alcazar-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciudad de Segovia: the Aqueduct and the Alcázar — circular&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.6 km route that takes just over an hour, offering iconic views of the city's monuments. The footpaths along the Eresma and Clamores River valleys also provide pleasant, easy walks.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Segovia?
A: Many of the hiking routes around Segovia are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular circular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153091765/ciudad-de-segovia-the-aqueduct-and-the-alcazar-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciudad de Segovia: the Aqueduct and the Alcázar — circular&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153077335/rio-eresma-with-views-of-the-alcazar-de-segovia-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Eresma with views of the Alcázar de Segòvia — circular&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the Eresma River.

Q: Are there any hikes that offer views of Segovia's historic monuments?
A: Absolutely. Many trails are designed to showcase Segovia's rich history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153091765/ciudad-de-segovia-the-aqueduct-and-the-alcazar-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciudad de Segovia: the Aqueduct and the Alcázar — circular&lt;/a&gt; route provides excellent views of the Aqueduct and Alcázar. The Segovia City Walls Walk also offers panoramic vistas of the Alcázar and Cathedral, framed by the river valleys.

Q: Where can I find trails with waterfalls or dramatic natural features near Segovia?
A: For dramatic natural features, head to the Hoces del Río Duratón Natural Park, known for its stunning limestone gorges. Within the Valsaín pine forests, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2714437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boca del Asno Gorge and Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, a beautiful area along the Eresma River.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I see while hiking in Segovia?
A: The region is rich in wildlife. In Hoces del Río Duratón Natural Park, you can often spot griffon vultures soaring overhead. Sierra de Guadarrama National Park is home to over 130 bird species and diverse mammals, offering excellent opportunities for wildlife observation.

Q: Are there challenging mountain hikes in the Segovia area?
A: Yes, for more challenging mountain hikes, the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park is an excellent choice. It offers demanding routes, including the ascent of Peñalara, the highest peak at 2,428 meters, featuring glacial lagoons and dense pine forests.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Segovia?
A: The trails in Segovia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 190 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from river gorges to mountain peaks, and the seamless blend of natural beauty with historical sites like the Aqueduct and Alcázar.

Q: Are there hiking routes that incorporate historical sites beyond the city center?
A: Yes, several routes connect natural beauty with history. The Aqueduct's Ancient Roots Trail, starting in Revenga, is a 12 km woodland path that follows the historical route of the Roman reservoir to the city's iconic aqueduct. The Vía Verde del Eresma, a repurposed railway track, also offers a journey through rural charm and past an old Roman bridge.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Segovia?
A: Segovia offers hiking opportunities year-round, but spring and autumn are particularly pleasant with mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes ideal, while winter offers unique snowy landscapes in the higher elevations of the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: How can I reach hiking trails around Segovia using public transport?
A: Many trails, especially those closer to the city like the Eresma and Clamores River Valley paths, are accessible directly from Segovia. For trails further afield, such as those in the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, local bus services connect Segovia with surrounding towns that serve as trailheads. It's advisable to check specific bus schedules for your chosen destination.

Q: Are there less crowded hiking options near Segovia?
A: For a quieter experience, consider exploring trails within the Hoces del Río Duratón Natural Park or the more extensive routes in the Sierra de Guadarrama National Park, which offer vast natural spaces. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1152524228/pinar-de-la-acebeda-y-el-berrueco-circular-por-el-acueducto-en-revenga-parque-natural-sierra-norte-de-guadarrama" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinar de la Acebeda y El Berrueco — circular&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, leads through pine forests and past the aqueduct in a more secluded setting.
tips:
The Segovia Aqueduct is an impressive architectural structure that dates back to the 1st century AD. C. and was built by the Romans during the occupation of Hispania. Its main function was to supply water to the city of Segovia, transporting it from the mountains to the urban center. The aqueduct carries the waters of the Fuenfría spring, located in the mountains about 17 kilometers from the city, to the center of Segovia. The water is collected in a cistern called El Caserón and then taken through an ashlar canal to a second tower known as Casa de Aguas. After traveling more than 15 km, you arrive at the city.
The Segovia Aqueduct is a heraldic symbol of the city and one of the most impressive works that the Romans left in their vast empire. If you ever have the opportunity to visit, I recommend that you do so. It's really amazing! 😊🏛️

Built between the 16th and 18th centuries, the Holy Cathedral Church of Our Lady of the Assumption and San Frutos is part of the late Gothic period. It is one of the latest Gothic cathedrals in Spain and Europe. It was built in the 16th century, specifically between the years 1525 and 1768.

The Alcázar is shown as the symbol of the old city of Segovia, a World Heritage Site by Unesco.
Its rooms were the resting place of Alfonso X el Sabio (1221-1284), the astronomer king. From them came out, on December 13, 1474, Isabella the Catholic to be proclaimed. The wake mass for the marriage of Felipe II and Ana de Austria (1570) was celebrated in its chapel.
Former 16th Century Royal Mint, now a museum.
